mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and impending recession risk, triggering institutional risk-off positioning and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances following the trigger activation, or the signal shows no statistical edge over a random baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in consumer credit outstanding and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand and household balance sheet health, supporting corporate earnings growth and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample rolling windows, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects supply chain normalization and capacity utilization expansion, which lowers unit costs and boosts corporate profit margins, driving positive equity valuations.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases consistently coincide with negative SPX forward returns in more than 60% of observations, or the signal exhibits no predictive power across multiple economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth falling below 1.5 percent signals economic stagnation and weakening corporate revenue trajectories, which compresses earnings multiples and depresses equity prices.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral in more than 65% of periods following the GDP growth threshold breach, demonstrating the trigger lacks directional predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous elevated consumer price inflation and restrictive federal funds rates compress real yields and equity risk premiums, forcing multiple contraction and generating negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The high-inflation high-rate combination does not produce negative 5-day forward returns more often than chance, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for volatility regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects healthy labor demand and wage growth, supporting consumer spending and corporate revenue expansion over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth trend shows no predictive power for 60-day SPX returns, with Rank IC remaining below 0.02 across all rolling estimation windows and forward periods.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1.5 million units signals strong construction activity and consumer confidence, driving positive equity returns through wealth effects and sectoral earnings growth.
failure_condition: HOUST remains above 1500 while SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observations, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in out-of-sample testing.
